data.task:
ATOM: Freeze the dead `playbooks/` base-persona genre (symmetric to the spike retirement, ADR-0030). This is a FOUNDER-AUTHORIZED verified cut (GO PLAYBOOKS). Loader was retired by ADR-0026 and the directory's own README states 'the retired loader no longer reads this directory.' Verified: NO live code under packages/core/src or packages/daemon/src reads these skeleton .md files.

DO THESE FOUR THINGS:

1. FREEZE (move, do NOT delete) the four skeleton files from `packages/personas/base/playbooks/` to a new frozen-history home `cocoder/zArchive/playbooks/` (symmetric to `cocoder/zArchive/spikes/`): `drift-audit.md`, `new-primary.md`, `new-primary-tech-stack.md`, `README.md`. Use `git mv` is NOT allowed (you do not run git); just move the files with the filesystem. After the move, `packages/personas/base/playbooks/` must no longer exist.

2. DROP THE GENRE FROM THE DIRECTORY MODEL. Find and remove the `playbooks/` base genre entry from: (a) ADR-0008 topology (`cocoder/decisions/0008-repository-topology.md`) — amend NON-DESTRUCTIVELY in the same shape ADR-0030 used for spikes (read `cocoder/decisions/0030-retire-spike-genre.md` first as the template), do not rewrite history, add a pointer that the genre is retired and frozen; (b) the ARCHITECTURE.md Directory Layout tree if it lists base `playbooks/`; (c) any AGENTS.md that lists it as a live genre. ONLY touch genuine LIVE references to the base genre — leave historical owner-map docs (`docs/onboarding-rebuild-ownermap.md`, `docs/drift-audit-ownermap.md`, `docs/orchestration-contract-ownership.md`) as historical record; they reference a stale `packages/n/...` path that no longer exists and document past audits, so do not rewrite them unless a reference would now actively mislead a reader about current live structure (if so, add a brief 'historical' marker rather than deleting).

3. RECORD A NEW APPEND-ONLY ADR `cocoder/decisions/0032-retire-playbooks-genre.md` (next free number; modeled exactly on ADR-0030's shape — read it first). It non-destructively amends ADR-0008's topology to retire the base `playbooks/` genre, explains the loader was retired by ADR-0026 and the skeletons are frozen to `cocoder/zArchive/playbooks/` as design history. Add the README index entry in `cocoder/decisions/README.md`.

4. DO NOT CONFLATE with the LIVE code module `packages/core/src/playbooks/` (the p1-p6 phase-executor / drift inventory, imported by `packages/core/src/drift/read-reality.ts`). That is a DIFFERENT thing and STAYS UNTOUCHED. The cut is ONLY the base-persona skeleton genre `packages/personas/base/playbooks/`.

ACCEPTANCE / MUST STAY GREEN (run these and report exit codes + output):
- `pnpm -F @cocoder/personas test`
- `node scripts/check-topology.mjs`
- `pnpm -r typecheck`
- any `scripts/proof-*.mjs` that reference playbooks/topology (at minimum `scripts/proof-drift-audit.mjs` if present)

MUST NOT BREAK: no load-bearing safeguard weakened; the live `packages/core/src/playbooks/` module untouched; behavior of the onboarding/drift priorities unchanged (they already run as ordinary Oscar priorities). Report the exact diff (files moved, files edited, new ADR) and all command outputs.

data.reason: Dead base playbooks/ genre frozen to zArchive (not deleted), ADR-0032 non-destructively amends ADR-0008, live core/src/playbooks untouched, all live refs reconciled; typecheck+topology+personas(22/22)+drift-audit proof all green.

data.outcome: ✅ COMMITTED on `main` — 2 commit(s) on the active branch (no landing step; work is on the branch by construction). Committed out-of-lane (flagged, NOT held back): ARCHITECTURE.md, cocoder/decisions/0008-repository-topology.md, cocoder/decisions/0020-primary-root-audit.md, cocoder/decisions/README.md, cocoder/priorities/drift-audit.md, cocoder/priorities/new-primary-root.md, cocoder/priorities/surface-reduction.md, cocoder/decisions/0032-retire-playbooks-genre.md, cocoder/zArchive/playbooks/README.md, cocoder/zArchive/playbooks/drift-audit.md, cocoder/zArchive/playbooks/new-primary-tech-stack.md, cocoder/zArchive/playbooks/new-primary.md.
